# playwright-scenarios

> Claude Code plugin marketplace for scenario-driven Playwright testing — record scenarios by driving a browser, audit them against the live site, and generate tests in the configured language and framework (default: Kotlin + Kotest StringSpec with Playwright-for-Java).

This repository is a Claude Code plugin marketplace (no application source). It ships a single plugin, `playwright-scenarios`, that lets JVM projects (and, over time, other stacks) author browser-driven test scenarios as flat markdown files, audit those scenarios against the live site, and translate them into test classes. Per-project behavior is driven by `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`.

## Docs

### README (key sections)

**Plugin purpose**
Author browser-driven scenarios as markdown, audit them against the live site, and generate tests.

**Commands**

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/record-scenario [url] [name]` | Launch Playwright codegen, capture a real user flow, and write a scenario markdown file to `<scenario_dir>/record/<name>.md`. Optionally accepts a Start URL so the browser opens straight to it. |
| `/crawl-site <start-url> [description] [--depth=N] [--max-scenarios=N]` | Read-only crawl of a site. Accepts a natural-language description ("focus on checkout flow") to guide scope. A bare URL crawls with defaults (depth 1, max 10 scenarios, no filtering). Emits scenarios to `<scenario_dir>/crawl/`. |
| `/doc-to-scenarios <path> [--skip-evaluation]` | Convert any document into scenario markdown files under `<scenario_dir>/convert/`. |
| `/review-scenario [names...]` | Audit scenarios across `<scenario_dir>/{record,crawl,convert}/` against the live site and apply improvements. A bare partition name scopes the review to that partition. |
| `/scenario-to-tests [names...] [--dry-run]` | Generate tests (defaults: Kotlin + Kotest StringSpec with Playwright-for-Java) at `<test_dir>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt`. `--dry-run` writes files but skips the test run. |
| `/scenario-status [description]` | Health dashboard grouped by partition: review dates, test status, pass/fail, coverage gaps. Optional natural-language description biases the rendering. |
| `/generate-fixture <source \| interactive> [--name=N]` | Scaffold a JSON fixture file from a scenario, document, or interactive prompts. |
| `/playwright-scenarios-config` | View or update per-project settings in `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`. Also the recovery path for malformed config files. |
| `/scaffold-base-test` | Generate a Kotlin `BasePageTest` so generated tests have a base class to extend. Prompts for `/reset` endpoint, lifecycle scope, and browser. Persists `base_test_class` in the config. Currently `kotlin` + `kotest-stringspec` only. Auto-offered by `loading-config` when no base class is found in the project. |

**Skills**

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `loading-config` | Reads `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md` and bootstraps it on first use; invoked at the start of every command. Scaffolds `<scenario_dir>/{record,crawl,convert}/` and `<test_dir>/{record,crawl,convert}/` on first run. |
| `authoring-scenarios` | Flat-markdown scenario format reference used whenever Claude hand-writes or edits a scenario markdown file. Supports 13 extended tags including `**Fixture:**`, `**Iframe:**`, `**Intercept:**`, `**Cookie:**`, `**Storage:**`, `**Device:**`. |
| `evaluate-doc` | Reads a QA document and produces a structured testability report. Advisory only — never writes scenario files. |
| `fixture-format` | Defines the canonical JSON fixture format shared across all generators. |
| `debugging-scenarios` | Guides troubleshooting when generated tests fail — iframe detection, selector drift, timing, stale fixtures. |
| `scaffold-base-test` | Renders the Kotlin `BasePageTest` template into the consuming project. Owns the three customization prompts (reset endpoint, lifecycle scope, browser) and the file write. Caller persists `base_test_class`. |

**Host project setup (Kotlin + Kotest default)**
1. `testImplementation("com.microsoft.playwright:playwright:...")` and `testImplementation("io.kotest:kotest-runner-junit5")` in `build.gradle.kts`.
2. Gradle tasks `recordScenario` (wraps `com.microsoft.playwright.CLI codegen`, driven by `-Purl=` / `-Pout=` properties) and `installPlaywrightBrowsers` (runs `CLI install`, required once before first recording).
3. `playwright-cli` binary (via `npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest` or `npx playwright-cli`). Required by `/review-scenario`, `/scenario-to-tests`, and `/crawl-site`. `/record-scenario` uses Gradle codegen instead.
4. Scenario directory — configured via `scenario_dir` (default `src/test/scenarios/`). Three command-keyed subdirectories live underneath: `record/`, `crawl/`, `convert/`. The bootstrap creates them. Scenarios are canonical from the moment a creation command writes them; users hand-edit or delete in place if they want.
5. A base test class extending Kotest's `StringSpec` that owns the Playwright browser lifecycle. Auto-detected and persisted as `base_test_class` in the config; if none exists, `loading-config` offers to scaffold one via `/scaffold-base-test`.

**Configuration** (`.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`)

```markdown
---
scenario_dir: src/test/scenarios
test_dir: src/test/kotlin/com/example/qa/scenarios
test_language: kotlin
test_framework: kotest-stringspec
# Optional, advanced:
# source_root: src/test/kotlin
# base_test_class: com.example.qa.BasePageTest
---
```

Four required fields (`scenario_dir`, `test_dir`, `test_language`, `test_framework`), two optional (`source_root`, `base_test_class`). Currently supported for `/scenario-to-tests`: `kotlin` + `kotest-stringspec` only. Other values accepted and persisted but gated.

**Workflow**
1. Seed: `/record-scenario checkout-flow` writes to `<scenario_dir>/record/checkout-flow.md`. `/crawl-site https://example.com` writes to `<scenario_dir>/crawl/`. `/doc-to-scenarios path/to/doc.md` writes to `<scenario_dir>/convert/`.
2. (Optional) Hand-edit or delete scenarios in place.
3. Review: `/review-scenario checkout-flow` validates claims against the live site and rewrites the markdown in place. Pass a partition name (`record`, `crawl`, `convert`) to scope.
4. Generate: `/scenario-to-tests checkout-flow` emits `<test_dir>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt`.

---

### CLAUDE.md

```markdown
# playwright-scenarios

Claude Code plugin marketplace. No application source — just plugin definitions under `plugins/`.

## Layout

- `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — marketplace manifest (lists plugins, versions, metadata).
- `plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` — per-plugin manifest.
- `plugins/<name>/commands/` — slash commands (markdown with YAML frontmatter).
- `plugins/<name>/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` — skills (auto-loaded by trigger description).

## Version bumps

When bumping a plugin version, update both `plugin.json` and the matching entry in `marketplace.json`, then add a `CHANGELOG.md` entry.

## Testing plugin changes

No automated tests. Validate by installing the plugin locally and running the commands against a real host project.

## Plugin runtime config

The `playwright-scenarios` plugin reads per-project settings from `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md` (YAML frontmatter: `scenario_dir`, `test_dir`, `test_language`, `test_framework`). Every command starts by invoking the `loading-config` skill, which prompts the user and creates the file on first use. Use `/playwright-scenarios-config` to re-prompt.
Optional advanced fields: `source_root`, `base_test_class` — auto-inferred and persisted; not prompted at bootstrap.

## Config file write authority

Only `loading-config` and `/playwright-scenarios-config` write `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`. Other skills return values; their callers persist. Single-writer prevents racy/partial updates and keeps the malformed-recovery path well-defined.

## Skill error contract

Skills return short structured error codes (e.g. `MALFORMED_CONFIG: <reason>`, `UNSUPPORTED_COMBO: <combo>`, `TARGET_EXISTS: <path>`) and let the calling command produce the user-facing message. Keeps messaging in one place per command and prevents drift between sibling docs.

## Doc propagation

When adding or changing a command, skill, or config field, update all of: README.md, CHANGELOG.md, llms.txt, llms-full.txt, plus the matching page under `website/playwright-scenarios/docs/` (e.g. `commands.md`, `terminology.md`). When adding a new command, also extend the explicit trigger list inside `loading-config`'s SKILL `description` field — the skill keeps working without it (fuzzy match), but the list is what other docs cite as the canonical command roster.

## Website docs (Zensical)

Pages live under `website/playwright-scenarios/docs/`. Nav order is set in `website/playwright-scenarios/zensical.toml` — new pages must be added to the `nav = [...]` array or they won't appear in the sidebar. Avoid backticks, dots, slashes, and parens in `##`/`###` headings if anything cross-links to them — slugs become fragile. When a command and a skill share a name (e.g. `### /scaffold-base-test` and `### scaffold-base-test`), they slugify to the same anchor; disambiguate with `attr_list` (`### scaffold-base-test {: #scaffold-base-test-skill }`). In Mermaid diagrams, never use `(...)` inside `[...]` node labels — it silently breaks rendering. Strip the parenthetical or move it to surrounding prose.

## External dependency

`/review-scenario`, `/scenario-to-tests`, and `/crawl-site` require the `playwright-cli` skill (not shipped by this marketplace). It lives at `~/.claude/skills/playwright-cli/` and wraps `@playwright/cli` (`npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest`).

## Source partitions

Scenario creation commands write to one of three partitions: `<scenario_dir>/{record,crawl,convert}/`. Generated tests mirror at `<test_dir>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt`. Use `<command>` (not `<partition>`) as the placeholder in path templates everywhere. The scenario in its partition is the canonical artifact; users hand-edit or delete in place before `/review-scenario`.

## Scenario format extensions

`authoring-scenarios` supports extended tags beyond the base Action/Expected format: `**Fixture:**`, `**Prerequisite:**`, `**Assert throughout:**`, `**Expected failure:**`, `**Expected (regex):**`, `**Iframe:**`, `**Branch:**`, `**Intercept:**`, `**Cookie:**`, `**Storage:**`, `**Device:**`, `**Timeout:**`, `**Cleanup:**`. See the skill for semantics. `/review-scenario` preserves these during rewrites.
```

---

## Plugin manifests

### .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://anthropic.com/claude-code/marketplace.schema.json",
  "name": "playwright-scenarios",
  "description": "Record, review, and generate Playwright tests from human-readable scenario markdown",
  "owner": { "name": "mattbobambrose" },
  "plugins": [
    {
      "name": "playwright-scenarios",
      "source": "./plugins/playwright-scenarios",
      "description": "Author browser-driven scenarios as markdown, audit them against the live site, and generate JVM Playwright + Kotest tests",
      "version": "0.8.0",
      "author": { "name": "mattbobambrose" },
      "category": "testing",
      "keywords": ["playwright", "testing", "kotest", "jvm", "kotlin", "e2e", "codegen"],
      "license": "MIT",
      "repository": "https://github.com/mattbobambrose/playwright-scenarios"
    }
  ]
}
```

### plugins/playwright-scenarios/.claude-plugin/plugin.json

```json
{
  "name": "playwright-scenarios",
  "version": "0.8.0",
  "description": "Author browser-driven scenarios as markdown, audit them against the live site, and generate JVM Playwright + Kotest tests",
  "author": { "name": "mattbobambrose" }
}
```

---

## Commands

### /record-scenario

**Description:** Record a new website validation scenario by driving a real browser. Launches Playwright codegen, captures clicks/typing/assertions, then writes a scenario markdown file to `<SCENARIO_DIR>/record/<name>.md`. Accepts an optional Start URL so the codegen browser opens directly there instead of prompting.

**Arguments:** optional `url` (a token starting with `http://` or `https://` — used as the Start URL) and optional `name` (kebab-case scenario filename; inferred from recorded actions if omitted). Order doesn't matter; tokens are content-detected. No flags.

**Phases:**
0. **Load config.** Invoke `loading-config` to resolve `<SCENARIO_DIR>` and bootstrap if missing. Abort on `MALFORMED_CONFIG`.
1. **Determine scenario name and Start URL.** Validate name as kebab-case if supplied; otherwise defer inference to step 6. If a URL was supplied as an argument, use it as the Start URL; otherwise prompt — no default.
2. **Collision check.** If name is known and `<SCENARIO_DIR>/record/<name>.md` exists, ask before overwriting.
3. **Brief the user on Playwright Inspector controls** (Assert visibility / text / value toolbar buttons).
4. **Run the recorder.** `./gradlew recordScenario -Purl=<start-url> -Pout=build/recordings/<filename>.java` with a long timeout. If the browser binary is missing, prompt the user to run `./gradlew installPlaywrightBrowsers`.
5. **Read the emitted Java recording file.**
6. **Infer name** (if not supplied): 2–4 kebab-case words from the primary user intent; collision check against `<SCENARIO_DIR>/record/`; confirm with the user.
7. **Convert to scenario markdown.** The `authoring-scenarios` skill is the authoritative reference for format, voice, selector rules, grouping, and known gotchas. Peek at an existing file under `<SCENARIO_DIR>/record/` (if any) to match house style.
8. **Write** `<SCENARIO_DIR>/record/<name>.md`.
9. **Report** path and one-line summary. Tell the user the next step is to hand-edit if desired, then run `/review-scenario`. No auto-chain.

**Playwright Java API → scenario markdown:**

- `page.navigate(url)` → first bullet: `- **Action:** Navigate to <url>` (or fold into the `**URL:**` header if it matches).
- `page.getByRole(..., setName("X")).click()` → `- **Action:** Click "X"`.
- `page.getByLabel("Email").fill("foo@bar")` → `- **Email:** foo@bar` then `- **Action:** Enter email …`.
- `page.selectOption(...)` → `- **Action:** Select "<value>" from the <label> dropdown`.
- `page.setViewportSize(w, h)` → `- **Viewport:** <w>x<h>`.
- `assertThat(locator).isVisible()` → `- **Expected:** The <element description> is visible`.
- `assertThat(locator).hasText("X")` → `- **Expected:** The <element description> shows "X"`.
- `assertThat(locator).hasValue("X")` → `- **Expected:** The <field description> contains "X"`.

---

### /crawl-site

**Description:** Read-only crawl of a website to seed user-flow-oriented scenarios under `<SCENARIO_DIR>/crawl/`. Complementary to `/record-scenario` — useful when you want Claude to explore autonomously instead of driving the browser yourself.

**Arguments:** required `<start-url>`, plus optional natural-language description and flags: `--depth=N` (same-origin hops, default 1, clamp `[1, 3]`), `--max-scenarios=N` (cap on emitted scenarios, default 10). A bare URL crawls with defaults (depth 1, max 10 scenarios, no filtering).

**Safety:** strictly read-only. Navigates, scrolls, snapshots. Never fills forms, never clicks buttons whose text suggests state change.

**Phases:**
0. **Argument parse + load config + preflight.** `loading-config` resolves `<SCENARIO_DIR>` (abort on `MALFORMED_CONFIG`). Verify `playwright-cli`. Ensure `<SCENARIO_DIR>/crawl/` exists.
1. **Fetch and inventory the start page.** Open via `playwright-cli`, snapshot, classify links.
2. **Identify candidate user flows** — group by intent (nav / hero CTA / content / footer / auth-gate). Dedupe by destination URL.
3. **Walk each selected flow** (parallel subagents, batches of 5) via direct `playwright-cli goto` (still read-only).
4. **Write scenarios** to `<SCENARIO_DIR>/crawl/<kebab-name>.md` using the `authoring-scenarios` format. Include a provenance blockquote. Disambiguate name collisions with `-v2`/`-v3` suffix.
5. **Write crawl metadata** to `<SCENARIO_DIR>/crawl/.crawl-meta.json` for `/scenario-status` to use.
6. **Report** a summary table and next-step pointers (review files in place, optionally delete unwanted ones, run `/review-scenario`). Do **not** auto-chain into `/review-scenario`.

---

### /doc-to-scenarios

**Description:** Convert any document into scenario markdown files under `<SCENARIO_DIR>/convert/` with proper tag mapping.

**Arguments:** required `<path>`, plus flag: `--skip-evaluation` (skip the inline evaluate-doc pass).

**Phases:**
0. Load config, resolve `<SCENARIO_DIR>`. Ensure `<SCENARIO_DIR>/convert/` exists.
1. Read the source doc. Unless `--skip-evaluation`, run `evaluate-doc` and pause for user approval before converting.
2. For each `direct` / `needs-changes` / `extended-tag` test case: group by URL/fixture/flow into scenario files, map to Action/Expected pairs and extended tags, flag `needs-changes` items with inline warnings.
3. Generate kebab-case filenames, check for collisions under `<SCENARIO_DIR>/convert/`.
4. Write scenario files (parallel subagents, batches of 5) to `<SCENARIO_DIR>/convert/<name>.md`. Each includes a provenance blockquote.
5. Report: scenario table, skipped out-of-scope items, next steps (review in place, generate fixtures if referenced, run `/review-scenario`, run `/scenario-to-tests`). No auto-chain.

---

### /review-scenario

**Description:** Audit website validation scenarios across `<SCENARIO_DIR>/{record,crawl,convert}/` against the live site and apply improvements to the markdown.

**Arguments:** zero or more scenario names (without `.md`), or partition names (`record`, `crawl`, `convert`) to scope the review. No flags.

**Phases:**
0. **Argument parse + load config + preflight.** `loading-config` resolves `<SCENARIO_DIR>` (abort on `MALFORMED_CONFIG`). Verify `playwright-cli --version` or `npx --no-install playwright-cli --version` works; abort with install guidance if both fail.
1. **Determine scenarios.** Zero names → glob `<SCENARIO_DIR>/{record,crawl,convert}/*.md`. Partition names → glob that partition. Scenario names → look up across all partitions, prompting on cross-partition ambiguity. Skip `SCENARIOS.md` and `.crawl-meta.json`.
2. **Live-site verification (main thread).** For each scenario, open the URL via `playwright-cli`, walk the test cases, and collect findings tagged `broken` / `vague` / `missing-coverage` / `style`.
3. **Improvement generation + auto-apply (parallel subagents).** Launch in batches of at most 5. One subagent per scenario, given the original markdown, its Phase 2 findings, and up to two style-reference scenarios from `<SCENARIO_DIR>/{record,crawl,convert}/`. Rules: preserve title/URL unless broken, preserve extended tags, don't delete tests, fix broken claims, tighten vague expectations, at most one new test per missing-coverage finding, don't reformat for style unless flagged, don't emit test code.
4. **Summary report.** Per-scenario findings counts, applied changes, deliberately-skipped findings with reasons, as a table. No build/test command.

---

### /scenario-to-tests

**Description:** Generate tests at `<TEST_DIR>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt` from reviewed scenarios. Tests are partitioned by source command and by scenario.

**Arguments:** zero or more scenario names (without `.md`) or partition names (`record`, `crawl`, `convert`), plus `--dry-run` to skip the test execution.

**Phases:**
0. **Argument parse + load config + resolve + preflight.** `loading-config` resolves `<SCENARIO_DIR>`, `<TEST_DIR>`, `<TEST_LANGUAGE>`, `<TEST_FRAMEWORK>`, and (if set) `<SOURCE_ROOT>` / `<BASE_TEST_CLASS>`. Abort on `MALFORMED_CONFIG`. **Unsupported-combo guard** — if not `kotlin` + `kotest-stringspec`, abort. Resolve `SCENARIOS_PACKAGE` from `<TEST_DIR>` minus the source root. Resolve `BASE_TEST_CLASS` via config or glob; persist discovered class to config. Verify `playwright-cli` is callable.
1. **Determine scenarios** — same selection rules as `/review-scenario` (zero args / partition names / scenario names). For each, retain the source partition (`record`, `crawl`, or `convert`).
2. **Browser exploration (main thread).** Walk each scenario via `playwright-cli`; record errors, selectors, URLs. Screenshots go in `screenshots/<command>/`.
3. **Test generation (parallel subagents).** Batches of at most 5 concurrent. Each subagent receives scenario content, observations, partition, scenario-name, and resolved config. Subagents write the test file but don't run the build.
4. **Run tests.** Skipped entirely if `--dry-run`. Named scenarios → target `<SCENARIOS_PACKAGE>.<command>.<ClassName>` per scenario. Partition names → target `<SCENARIOS_PACKAGE>.<command>.*`. Zero args → target `<SCENARIOS_PACKAGE>.*`. Fix failures sequentially.

**Scenario-name → class-name conversion:** drop `.md`; treat `-`, `_`, `.` as separators; drop non-alphanumerics; title-case each word; concatenate; prefix `_` if starts with a digit; append `Test`. Examples: `login-invalid-credentials.md` → `LoginInvalidCredentialsTest`, `2fa-setup.md` → `_2faSetupTest`.

**Output path:** `<TEST_DIR>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt`. The `<scenario-name>/` directory is kebab-case verbatim and purely organizational on disk; the `.kt` file declares its package as `<SCENARIOS_PACKAGE>.<command>` (no `<scenario-name>` segment). IDEs may flag the directory-vs-package mismatch; compilation works because Kotest discovery is classpath-based.

**Kotlin + Kotest StringSpec generation rules:**
- Kotest StringSpec with `init` block.
- Extend `BASE_TEST_CLASS` if resolved; otherwise omit the extends and add a TODO.
- Class in `<SCENARIOS_PACKAGE>.<command>`, named by the conversion rule above.
- Written to `<TEST_DIR>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt`, creating the directory tree if needed.

---

### /generate-fixture

**Description:** Scaffold a JSON fixture file from a scenario's data bullets, a document's persona table, or interactive prompts.

**Arguments:** required `<source>` (scenario path, document path, or `interactive`), plus `--name=<fixture-name>`.

**Phases:**
0. Load config, ensure `<SCENARIO_DIR>/fixtures/` exists.
1. Extract data: from scenario (collect input data bullets), from document (find persona tables), or interactively (prompt for groups and fields).
2. Build JSON following the `fixture-format` skill schema (`_meta` + snake_case groups + leaf values matching DOM identifiers).
3. Check for collisions — offer overwrite, merge, or rename.
4. Write to `<SCENARIO_DIR>/fixtures/<name>.json`.
5. Report path, field count, and suggest updating the scenario's `**Fixture:**` tag.

---

### /scenario-status

**Description:** Health dashboard for all scenarios across the record / crawl / convert partitions — review dates, test file status, pass/fail, coverage gaps. Accepts an optional free-form English description that biases what the report emphasizes.

**Argument:** Everything after the command name, joined into one string, is the optional focus description (`<FOCUS>`). Examples: `/scenario-status focus on what's broken`, `/scenario-status one-paragraph executive summary`, `/scenario-status only the checkout-related scenarios`. Empty `<FOCUS>` → default full dashboard.

**Phases:**
0. Load config.
1. Inventory all scenarios under `<SCENARIO_DIR>/{record,crawl,convert}/*.md`. Track partition per file. Skip `SCENARIOS.md` and `.crawl-meta.json`.
2. Check review status via `git log -1` per file.
3. Check test file existence and staleness for `<TEST_DIR>/<command>/<scenario-name>/<ClassName>.kt` (scenario newer than test file = stale).
4. Parse most recent Gradle test report for pass/fail (optional — skipped if no report found). Match classes against `<SCENARIOS_PACKAGE>.{record,crawl,convert}.*`.
5. Coverage completeness: crawl depth (from `<SCENARIO_DIR>/crawl/.crawl-meta.json`), flow type coverage, conversion rate by partition, critical paths (from `.critical-paths.md`).
6. Print dashboard. With no `<FOCUS>`: tables grouped by partition + summary stats + recommended actions. With `<FOCUS>`: lead with a tailored 1–3-paragraph prose summary citing concrete numbers, then keep/condense/skip standard sections according to the focus, optionally filter the per-partition tables (e.g. "checkout-related scenarios"), and reorder the recommended actions to match. **Invariant in all modes:** every scenario reference is partition-tagged (record / crawl / convert) so the user always knows which command produced it — partition headers are kept (with `(no scenarios)` if empty), prose summaries name partitions when citing counts, recommended actions qualify scenario names with their partition.

---

### /playwright-scenarios-config

**Description:** View or update the playwright-scenarios per-project settings stored in `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`. Also the recovery path when that file is malformed.

**Steps:**
1. **Probe the config file directly** (don't start with `loading-config`, which would bounce back on malformed files and loop). Branch: missing → delegate to `loading-config` bootstrap; clean → continue; malformed → go to recovery.
   - **Malformed-recovery path**: print the broken file and a diagnosis, ask the user to confirm overwrite, delete (or back up) the file, then invoke `loading-config` to bootstrap a fresh one.
2. Show current values in a compact table (four required fields plus `source_root` / `base_test_class` if present).
3. Re-prompt each field in three rounds (round 1: `scenario_dir`, `test_language`; round 2: `test_dir` with language-aware suggestions; round 3: `test_framework` scoped to the chosen language), pre-filling the current value as the first option. Don't re-prompt for `source_root` or `base_test_class`.
4. Rewrite the YAML frontmatter, preserving any hand-written markdown body below and preserving the optional fields if they were present.
5. Report which fields changed.

---

### /scaffold-base-test

**Description:** Generate a Kotlin `BasePageTest` class so `/scenario-to-tests` has a base class to extend. Without one, generated tests are emitted with no `extends` clause and a TODO comment per file. One-shot setup; the resulting class is registered as `base_test_class` and reused thereafter. Currently `kotlin` + `kotest-stringspec` only.

**Steps:**
1. Load config via `loading-config`. Abort on `MALFORMED_CONFIG`.
2. Unsupported-combo guard: stop if `test_language` ≠ `kotlin` or `test_framework` ≠ `kotest-stringspec`.
3. Already-configured guard: stop if `base_test_class` is non-empty in config (tell the user to clear it first).
4. Hand off to the `scaffold-base-test` skill (passing `<TEST_LANGUAGE>`, `<TEST_FRAMEWORK>`, `<TEST_DIR>`, `<SOURCE_ROOT>`). The skill prompts for three customizations, renders the template, and writes the file. Returns the FQN, target path, and the user's choices.
5. Persist `base_test_class: <fqn>` to the YAML frontmatter of `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md` (preserve all other lines and the markdown body verbatim).
6. Report the file path, FQN, and chosen customizations.

**Auto-invocation path:** `loading-config`'s base-test-class discovery offers to scaffold one when zero candidates are found in the project. The user can accept (skill runs identically; loading-config persists the FQN) or decline (loading-config falls through to its existing warning behavior).

---

## Skills

### loading-config

**Description:** Load per-project configuration for the playwright-scenarios plugin from `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`. Triggers automatically at the start of every playwright-scenarios command. Prompts the user for the four required fields (`scenario_dir`, `test_dir`, `test_language`, `test_framework`) on first run and persists them. Additional optional fields (`source_root`, `base_test_class`) are auto-inferred and persisted on disambiguation.

**Config schema:** YAML frontmatter with four required fields (`scenario_dir`, `test_dir`, `test_language`, `test_framework`) and two optional advanced fields (`source_root`, `base_test_class`), plus a markdown body (free-form human notes). All four required fields must be present and non-empty or the file is considered malformed.

**Resolution procedure:**
1. Read `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md`.
2. Present and parses cleanly with all four required fields → return resolved values.
3. Present but malformed → return the structured error `MALFORMED_CONFIG: <problem>` to the caller; never overwrite from this skill. `/playwright-scenarios-config` has a dedicated recovery path that bypasses this skill.
4. Missing → bootstrap in three rounds:
   - **Round 1** (one `AskUserQuestion` call): `scenario_dir`, `test_language`.
   - **Round 2** (second call): `test_dir` with language-aware suggestions — e.g. `kotlin` → `src/test/kotlin/.../scenarios`; `typescript`/`python` → `tests/scenarios`.
   - **Round 3** (third call): `test_framework` scoped to the language — e.g. `kotlin` → `kotest-stringspec` / `junit5`; `typescript` → `playwright-test` / `jest`. Prevents invalid pairings.

**Source-root inference (used by `/scenario-to-tests`):** if `source_root` is set, use it verbatim; otherwise match `<test_dir>` against known prefixes in order (`src/test/kotlin`, `src/test/java`, `src/test/scala`, `src/test/groovy`, `src/test/<lang>`, `test/kotlin`, `test/java`, `test/<lang>`, `tests/<lang>`, `src/test`). No match → abort asking the user to set `source_root`.

**Base-test-class discovery:** if `base_test_class` is set, use it; otherwise glob for `Base*Test.*`, `*TestBase.*`, `Abstract*Test.*`, `*PageTest.*`, or a class with `StringSpec`/`FunSpec` + `Playwright.create()`/`browser`/`page` markers. Exactly one match → use silently and persist. Multiple → prompt to pick and persist. None → offer to scaffold one via the `scaffold-base-test` skill (default Yes); if user accepts, run the skill and persist the returned FQN; if user declines, warn and continue without `extends`.

**Non-responsibilities:**
- Does not validate that `test_dir` exists on disk.
- Does not hot-reload; callers must re-invoke to pick up edits made mid-session.
- Does not prompt for build tool or Playwright binding; those are derived internally from `test_language` (Kotlin/Java → Gradle + Playwright-for-Java; TypeScript → npm + Playwright Test; Python → pip + Playwright-Python).
- Does not recover from malformed config — `/playwright-scenarios-config` owns that.

---

### authoring-scenarios

**Description:** Conventions for hand-writing or editing scenario markdown files in the project's scenario directory — the flat format that `/review-scenario` audits and `/scenario-to-tests` consumes. Scenario directory configured in `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md` (default `src/test/scenarios/`).

**File structure:**

```markdown
# <Title Case Title>

**URL:** <start path or full url>

<Optional one-line description of the flow.>

## Test 1: <short imperative description>

- **Action:** <what the user does>
- **Expected:** <what should be true after the action>
```

**Required:** top-level `# <Title>`, `**URL:**` line, one or more `## Test N:` sections numbered from 1, at least one Action and one Expected bullet per test.

**Optional:** `**Viewport:** <w>x<h>`; input-data bullets (`- **Email:** foo@bar.com`) referenced by a later Action.

**Voice:** imperative Actions, descriptive Expecteds.

**Selectors:** preserve concrete role+name / labels / visible text so the auditor can match live DOM. Avoid abstract phrases when a role+name exists.

**Grouping:** one continuous flow → one Test; same form with multiple inputs → one Test per input set; re-navigation usually marks a new Test.

**Gotchas:**
- Native HTML5 validation tooltips are not DOM — express as `- **Expected:** The email field reports a missing-value validation error`; the generator translates to `validity.valueMissing` / `validity.typeMismatch`.
- New-tab / cross-origin links — express as `- **Expected:** The '<label>' link points to <url>`; the generator checks `getAttribute("href")` instead of clicking.

**After editing:** for non-trivial changes, suggest the user run `/review-scenario <name>` to re-validate claims before regenerating tests.

---

### evaluate-doc

**Description:** Evaluate any document against the playwright-scenarios plugin's capabilities. Reports what converts directly, what needs changes, and what's out of scope. Advisory only — reads and reports, never writes scenario files.

**When to use:** User provides a doc and asks "can we test this?", "what would need to change?", or "evaluate this document."

**Analysis procedure:**
1. **Inventory** — extract every discrete test case, acceptance criterion, or testable assertion from the document.
2. **Classify** each as `direct` (converts to Action/Expected with no changes), `needs-changes` (expressible but document needs modification), `extended-tag` (needs Fixture, Prerequisite, Assert throughout, Expected failure, or Expected (regex)), or `out-of-scope` (cross-run comparison, visual regression, a11y, performance, network-layer).
3. **Flag document-level issues** — missing iframe/architecture boundaries, display text without DOM identifiers, assumed behaviors needing live-site verification, phase-numbering drift risk, mixed test/toolchain concerns, missing fixture data, missing scope boundaries.
4. **Map extended tags** — match document patterns to the appropriate scenario tag.
5. **Suggest fixes** — for each `needs-changes` item, produce a specific actionable suggestion.

**Output:** Summary paragraph → test-case table (# | Summary | Category | Tag/Issue | Suggested change) → document-level issues → out-of-scope items with alternatives → conversion roadmap (prioritized next steps).

**Does not:** write scenario files, open a browser, generate test code, or modify the input document.

---

### fixture-format

**Description:** Defines the standardized fixture file format (JSON) for typed test data shared across all test generators (Kotlin, TypeScript, Python). Triggered when Claude creates or modifies files under a `fixtures/` directory.

**Format:** JSON files at `<SCENARIO_DIR>/fixtures/<name>.json`. Schema: `_meta` (name, description, created, source) + domain-specific groups (snake_case keys) + leaf values matching DOM identifiers. Arrays for multi-select fields, strings for text, numbers for numeric inputs. The `**Branch:**` tag overrides individual fields at runtime without modifying the fixture file.

**Generator usage:** Kotlin reads via `kotlinx.serialization`, TypeScript uses `import fixture from '...'`, Python uses `json.load()`. All read the same `.json` file.

---

### debugging-scenarios

**Description:** Troubleshoot failing tests generated by `/scenario-to-tests`. Triggered when the user says tests are failing or generated tests don't match expected behavior.

**Diagnosis order (stop at first match):**
1. **Iframe not declared** — every selector fails; check for missing `**Iframe:**` tag. Fix: add the tag.
2. **Selector drift** — some selectors fail; element text/roles changed on the live site. Fix: update scenario, re-run `/review-scenario`.
3. **Timing / race condition** — tests pass inconsistently; element exists but isn't interactable. Fix: add an Expected assertion before the failing Action, or add `**Timeout:**`.
4. **Cross-origin / navigation boundary** — tests fail after an origin change. Fix: add `**Iframe:** none` or flag as needing manual test adjustment.
5. **Stale fixture data** — form rejects entered data (email taken, wrong format). Fix: use timestamped emails, update fixture values.
6. **Generated code structure wrong** — compile errors, wrong package. Fix: verify `test_dir`, `source_root`, `base_test_class` via `/playwright-scenarios-config`.

---

### scaffold-base-test

**Description:** Render the Kotlin `BasePageTest` template and write it to disk. Owns the three customization prompts (reset endpoint, lifecycle scope, browser) and the variant rules that compose them with the canonical template. Invoked by `/scaffold-base-test` (explicit) and by `loading-config` (auto-offered when zero base-test-class candidates are found). Currently `kotlin` + `kotest-stringspec` only.

**Inputs:** `test_language`, `test_framework`, `test_dir`, `source_root` from the caller.

**Guards:** abort if `test_language` ≠ `kotlin` / `test_framework` ≠ `kotest-stringspec`, or if the target file already exists. Never overwrites.

**Procedure:**
1. **Resolve target path and package.** `base_dir` = parent of `test_dir`; `target_file` = `<base_dir>/BasePageTest.kt`; `package_name` = `<base_dir>` with `<source_root>` prefix stripped, `/` → `.`. Empty package → emit no `package` line.
2. **Three prompts** (single `AskUserQuestion`): `/reset` endpoint (Yes/No, default Yes), lifecycle (per-spec/per-test, default per-spec), browser (Chromium/Firefox/Webkit, default Chromium).
3. **Render** the canonical template (Yes-reset, per-spec, Chromium) and apply variant rules: drop `resetServerState()` and its imports if `/reset = No`; swap `beforeSpec`/`afterSpec` for `beforeTest`/`afterTest` (and swap `Spec` import for `TestCase` + `TestResult`) if `lifecycle = per-test`; substitute browser method (`chromium()` / `firefox()` / `webkit()`). Imports stay alphabetically sorted in a single flat block.
4. **Write** the file via the Write tool, creating intermediate directories as needed.
5. **Return** `{fqn, target_file, choices}` to the caller. The caller persists `base_test_class` — this skill never edits the config.

**Non-responsibilities:**
- Does not write to `.claude/playwright-scenarios.local.md` (caller's job).
- Does not regenerate or overwrite existing `BasePageTest.kt` (caller deletes first).
- Does not add Playwright/Kotest dependencies to `build.gradle.kts`.
- Does not support languages or frameworks other than Kotlin + kotest-stringspec; additional template variants will land alongside `/scenario-to-tests` generation paths for those stacks.

---

## Website docs

User-facing documentation lives at `website/playwright-scenarios/docs/` and is rendered by Zensical. Nav order is configured in `website/playwright-scenarios/zensical.toml`.

- **index** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/index.md`): Site landing page — elevator pitch, quick start, links to each section.
- **tutorial** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/tutorial.md`): Step-by-step walkthrough from installation to generated tests.
- **terminology** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/terminology.md`): Definitions for scenario, test case, draft, fixture, tag, generated test.
- **workflow** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/workflow.md`): The four paths to a reviewed scenario (LLM-authored, existing-doc migration, record, crawl) with Mermaid diagrams.
- **commands** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/commands.md`): Per-command and per-skill reference — signatures, arguments, flags, examples for all 9 commands and 6 skills.
- **capabilities** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/capabilities.md`): What the plugin can and can't test, with the full extended-tag table.
- **writing-docs** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/writing-docs.md`): Guidance for writing input documents that convert cleanly into scenarios.
- **troubleshooting** (`website/playwright-scenarios/docs/troubleshooting.md`): Setup-time and operational failures with Symptom→Cause→Fix entries (plugin install, config bootstrap, `playwright-cli`, browser binaries, host-project, non-Kotlin stacks, crawl results, URL reachability, scenario layout, `/scenario-status`).
